title: Burns Night: Poetry, Haggis, and Highland Fun!
story: Every January 25, people celebrate Burns Night to honor Robert Burns, Scotland's famous poet! Families gather for special dinners featuring haggis, a traditional Scottish dish made with meat and oats. Someone reads Burns' poems aloud, especially his funny verses about haggis. People wear kilts, play bagpipes, and toast with Scottish drinks while sharing stories and laughter.
activity_question: What traditional Scottish dish is featured at Burns Night dinners?
bonus_fact: Robert Burns wrote a famous poem called "Address to a Haggis" that people still recite today!

title: Wake Up and Smell the Irish Coffee!
story: Coffee gets a fancy Irish makeover every January 25 on National Irish Coffee Day! This warm drink mixes hot coffee with Irish whiskey, sugar, and fluffy whipped cream on top. Since kids can't have the whiskey part, many cafes make special "Irish-style" coffee drinks with caramel or vanilla flavoring instead. Families celebrate by trying new coffee shop treats or making their own creamy coffee drinks at home. Some people even learn about Irish culture while sipping their special beverages!

follow_up_question: What other countries might have their own special coffee traditions?

title: Pop Till You Drop Day!
story: Pop, pop, POP! Every January 25, people across the country celebrate National Bubble Wrap Day by doing exactly what you're thinking – popping those amazing little air bubbles! This squishy packaging material that protects our online orders has become a stress-busting superstar. People host bubble wrap stomping parties, create bubble wrap art, and even have popping contests to see who can pop the most bubbles in one minute. Some folks save up bubble wrap all year just for this day! Schools use it for science experiments, and offices let workers take "bubble breaks" to pop away their worries. It's the one day when playing with your packaging is totally encouraged!

title: Put Pen to Paper Day!
story: Grab a pen and get ready to write! National Handwriting Day happens every January 25, celebrating the lost art of writing by hand. People share photos of their prettiest cursive, practice their signatures, and write thank-you notes instead of typing them. Teachers organize handwriting contests while students show off their neatest letters and coolest doodles.
activity_question: What do people often share photos of on National Handwriting Day?

title: Tatiana Day: Russia's Student Holiday
story: Russian students throw the biggest party of the year on January 25 for Tatiana Day! This special holiday honors Saint Tatiana, the patron saint of students and universities. College kids across Russia celebrate by skipping classes, having huge parties, and giving gifts to their teachers. Many universities host concerts, festivals, and special meals. Students believe celebrating Tatiana Day brings good luck for their exams and studies throughout the year.
activity_question: What do Russian students traditionally do on Tatiana Day?

title: Going Nuts for Macadamias!

story: Timothy Thomas Fortune took a brave stand for equality on January 25, 1890. He founded the National Afro-American League in Chicago, one of America's earliest civil rights organizations. title: The Father of Modern Chemistry
story: A scientific superstar was born on January 25, 1627. Robert Boyle, known as the Father of Modern Chemistry, entered the world in Lismore, Ireland. This curious kid grew up to shake up science! Boyle's experiments with air pressure and gases led to big discoveries. He showed that air has weight and takes up space, ideas that changed how we understand our world.

bonus_fact: Boyle's Law, named after Robert, describes how gas pressure and volume are related.

story: A brilliant mind entered the world on January 25, 1736. Joseph-Louis Lagrange, born in Turin, Italy, would grow up to become a math superstar. title: The Birth of a Bossa Nova Legend
story: A musical genius entered the world in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Antônio Carlos Jobim, later known as Tom Jobim, was born in 1927 on January 25. This talented singer-songwriter would grow up to create the smooth, jazzy sounds of bossa nova music. Jobim's famous album Getz/Gilberto helped bring Brazilian rhythms to listeners around the globe.
